statement: Under the EU Treaties, Iceland would be entitled to a minimum of 6 seats in the European Parliament (the Treaty minimum for any member state). By comparison, Malta has 6 seats, Luxembourg has 6 seats, and Cyprus has 6 seats. The European Parliament has 720 seats total.
caveats: The exact number of seats would be determined by the European Council decision on Parliament composition, but the Treaty guarantees a minimum of 6.

related_entries.statement: In the EU Council, qualified majority voting (QMV) requires 55% of member states representing 65% of the EU population. Iceland, with approximately 390,000 inhabitants, would have the smallest population of any EU member state. For comparison, Malta (540,000) currently holds the smallest population share. Iceland would have minimal blocking power alone but could join coalitions of small states.

citations.report_title: Flott embætti í boði fyrir ESB ríki

claims.claim_text: Aðildarríki ESB eigi tilkall til ýmissa embætta innan stofnana sambandsins og sagan sýni að æðstu embættin séu gjarnan skipuð fyrrum ráðherrum og háttsettum embættismönnum.

citations.report_title: Þrír hópar sem myndu hagnast mest á aðild Íslands að Evrópusambandinu

claims.claim_text: Með ESB-aðild myndi Ísland fá fulltrúa í ráðherraráði, nefndum og Evrópuþinginu, líkt og önnur smáríki á borð við Möltu, Eistland eða Slóveníu.
